arts.ttl: 'Brilliant addition': Simon Jordan thinks Chelsea signing has been great despite making just six starts
Manchester United and Chelsea ‘considering move’ – €80 to €100m asking price rise, Crystal Palace look out of it
arts.sum: Chelsea, Manchester United and Crystal Palace are all showing interest in Benfica’s Tomás Araújo. Crystal Palace tried to sign the defender by tabling a €30m bid in the summer but it was rejected. On November 13th, Correio da Manhã said Oliver Glasner’s side are still chasing him. arts.ttl: Five players for your Fantasy team this week
arts.sum: Gameweek 14 is the first midweek of Premier League action and Tom Hill has picked out five players to include in your Fantasy Football team.

arts.ttl: Euro giants eyeing big names from Tottenham, Arsenal & Chelsea as potential transfer targets
arts.sum: Juventus are reportedly eyeing up a number of defensive transfer targets, including one of their former players in Tottenham’s Radu Dragusin. The 22-year-old spent five years on the books at Juve as a youngster, but only made four first-team appearances for the club before moving on. Tottenham ready to sign South American star! arts.ttl: Russell Martin believes Enzo Maresca has already overcome 'toughest challenge' at Chelsea
arts.sum: Southampton boss Russell Martin has praised the job Enzo Maresca is doing at Chelsea and admitted the pair are facing different challenges to what they experienced last season. Despite losing both league encounters to Leicester last term, Martin believes those experiences might offer insights into Maresca's tactics, though he now has a different squad quality to work with.

arts.ttl: Bidding war ERUPTS for unhappy Chelsea star
arts.sum: It has been reported in recent weeks that unless Chelsea manager Enzo Maresca starts playing Nkunku regularly in the biggest matches, he will push to leave the club in January. A bidding war has erupted in Saudi Arabia for unhappy Chelsea star Christopher Nkunku .

arts.ttl: Revealed: FIFA confirm pots for 2025 Club World Cup draw - with Chelsea, Man City and Real Madrid all set to feature in the United States tournament
arts.sum: The 2025 Club World Cup will be contested by 32 clubs from the six continental confederations and will be the first edition in this format to take place in the United States.
